thesis: Worldwide supplier of semiconductor test and burn-in solutions pivoting from EV/SiC automotive market to AI/data center opportunities. FOX family systems enable wafer-level burn-in for silicon carbide power semiconductors and now AI processors. Strong balance sheet with $31M cash and minimal debt, but currently unprofitable with negative FCF. High valuation (25x P/S) reflects AI-driven optimism.

drivers: AI processor testing becomes major revenue driver ($50M+ annually)
Silicon photonics orders materialize in FY2027
Return to profitability in FY2027 with expanding margins
Short squeeze amplifies upside from elevated short interest (15.58%)

risks: AI opportunity proves smaller or more competitive than expected
Customer delays push out production ramps
Semiconductor capex cycle downturn impacts demand
Company burns cash and needs to raise capital

base_case.assumptions: Turnaround takes longer than expected but progress continues; AI revenue grows but doesn't fully offset EV/SiC softness until late FY2027; Company achieves break-even by end of FY2027.

catalysts.event: Q3 FY2026 Earnings — First quarter of reinstated guidance execution
Q4 FY2026 Earnings — FY2027 guidance will drive stock direction

key_risks: Customer concentration risk — pivoting from EV/SiC to AI dependence
Unprofitability and cash burn during investment phase
High valuation vulnerable to execution misses
Competition from larger semiconductor test companies (Advantest, Teradyne)
Heavy insider selling signals lack of confidence
